channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: House passes nearly $1 trillion defense spending bill, adding to US debt of $36 trillion
item.description: The U.S. House passed a mostly bipartisan bill to spend nearly $1 trillion on national defense, adding to the already growing $36 trillion national debt.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Person accused of accosting Rep. Nancy Mace at Capitol pleads not guilty to assault charge
item.description: A person accused of accosting U.S. Rep. Nancy Mace in a Capitol Office building has pleaded not guilty to a misdemeanor assault charge.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Garland lauds 'principled' Wray for 'integrity and skill' after resignation announcement
item.description: AG Garland praises FBI Director Wray's multi-decade career as a civil servant and U.S. prosecutor, including U.S. assistant AG and head of the Justice Department's Criminal Division.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawmakers edge closer to deal on government funding
item.description: Lawmakers say they are edging closer to a deal to keep the government's lights on in nine days but are still trying to work out differences over disaster aid and potential add-ons to the bill. There’s some optimism in the Capitol that bill text could arrive as soon as this weekend as the Dec.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics: Wray To Go
item.description: the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump transition, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mace, activist dispute handshake that resulted in arrest
item.description: A disputed handshake is at the center of a Tuesday incident involving Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) that resulted in an Illinois man’s arrest, according to a police report and a witness who spoke to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New data reveals America has tens of thousands of noncitizens from US adversary with deportation orders
item.description: Tens of thousands of Chinese nationals are among the more than 1.4 million noncitizens with final orders of removal but who are not in ICE custody, according to new figures.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hegseth meets with moderate Sen. Collins as he lobbies for key votes in the Senate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pete Hegseth, President-elect Donald Trump’s nominee to lead the Defense Department, said he had a “wonderful conversation” with Maine Sen. Susan Collins on Wednesday as he pushed to win enough votes for confirmation.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New poll reveals what Americans think of Trump's transition decisions
item.description: Roughly half of Americans approve of how President-elect Trump is handling his transition to a second term in the White House, according to two new national polls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Newsom sets special election dates for vacant 32nd District Assembly seat
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom announced a special election date for the vacant 32nd District Assembly.
item.dc:creator: Jose Franco, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Trump-aligned committee chair pledges 'colonoscopy' of State Department spending
item.description: Rep. Brian Mast, who is expected to be the next House Foreign Affairs Committee chair, speaks with Fox News Digital about his goals for the panel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans looking for new ways to force through China crackdowns left out of yearly defense bill
item.description: After a number of China crackdown provisions failed to make it into the yearly defense bill, Republicans are working on ways to get them signed into law before the end of the year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mast blasts Blinken over 'tens of billions' of US taxpayer dollars sent to Taliban post-Afghanistan withdrawal
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken was grilled by the incoming chair of the House Foreign Relations Committee on "billions" of U.S. dollars sent to Taliban.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US inflation ticked up last month as some price pressures remain persistent
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Fueled by pricier used cars, hotel rooms and groceries, inflation in the United States moved slightly higher last month in the latest sign that some price pressures remain elevated.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump trolls Trudeau as 'governor' of Canada
item.description: "It was a pleasure to have dinner the other night with Governor Justin Trudeau of the Great State of Canada," Trump wrote in a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Making a $1B investment in the US? Trump pledges expedited permits — but there are hurdles
item.description: Much like other Trump plans, the idea is likely to run into regulatory and legislative hurdles, including a landmark law that requires federal agencies to consider the environmental impact before deciding on major projects.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Report on attempts to kill Trump urges Secret Service to limit protection of foreign leaders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A congressional task force investigating the attempts to kill Donald Trump during his presidential campaign is recommending changes to the Secret Service, including protecting fewer foreign leaders during the height of election season and considering moving the agency out of the Ho
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: James Comer predicts Biden will pardon his brother Jim
item.description: “I do think he’ll pardon other family members. He’s going to have to,” Comer said in the clip highlighted by Grabien.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Pete Hegseth, Tulsi Gabbard on Capitol Hill to get Senate support
item.description: Two of President-elect Donald Trump's most controversial cabinet nominees are on Capitol Hill this week.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Florida State Rep. Susan Valdes, representing parts of Hillsborough County, switches political parties
item.description: State Rep. Susan Valdes, who represents Florida's 64th District, including parts of Hillsborough County, switched political parties, the Florida House of Representatives announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bidders are back in court battling over the auction of Alex Jones' Infowars
item.description: A U.S. bankruptcy judge is hearing arguments for and against selling the show to The Onion, the satirical news site named the winning bidder. Host Alex Jones says the auction was rigged.
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court rejects challenge to Boston's school admissions policy
item.description: The court's action was the second time the justices declined to intervene in an admissions program based on geography since their 2023 ruling invalidating affirmative action in higher education.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Social Security beneficiaries at risk of delays amid GOP funding fight
item.description: Social Security beneficiaries could face longer wait times for service next year unless Congress agrees to the White House budget office’s request to increase funding for the Social Security Administration (SSA), the administration that runs the program is warning.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Congressman Torres blasts crime in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The cold-blooded shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in the heart of Manhattan shocked New Yorkers, including Congressman Ritchie Torres, leaving the lawmaker to pose the question: “What is happening in New York City?” Torres joined PIX on Politics to discuss city crim
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Minneapolis City Council’s budget committee reduces proposed property tax levy for 2025
item.description: Council members, citing concerns about elevated costs for constituents, approved a series of amendments to the mayor’s proposed budget which would reduce the levy hike by 1.4 percent.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Walz says he was 'a little surprised' by election loss
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), Vice President Kamala Harris' running mate, reflected on the Democratic Party's loss in the presidential election, saying he was "a little surprised" by the outcome.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Former national security officials urge caution on Gabbard
item.description: A group of nearly 100 former national security officials urged the Senate to “carefully scrutinize” former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), President-elect Trump’s nominee to serve as director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump nominates Rodney Scott to lead Customs and Border Protection
item.description: Rodney Scott at CBP and Caleb Vitello at ICE would work alongside Stephen Miller, who was named deputy chief of staff for policy and Tom Homan, also tapped to be a "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: Talking Sense: Coming together to solve tough problems
item.description: Mariah Levison and Rob Fersh help people find consensus on how to solve divisive issues, ranging from prison reform to what art should hang in the Minnesota Capitol. Learn how they do it in this MPR News Talking Sense conversation hosted by MPR News correspondent Catharine Richert.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis, Catharine Richert, Maja Beckstrom, and Annie Baxter, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy are bringing Trump’s DOGE to Capitol Hill
item.description: The leaders of the so-called “Department of Government Efficiency” are calling for large-scale layoffs of federal workers and the elimination of some federal agencies during Trump's second term.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
